subject: Human Resources
title: Pulse Surveys
description: A quick win: understand Pulse Surveys.

hook.text: Five questions. Every two weeks. Results in 24 hours. That's a pulse survey—fast, frequent, and actionable.

buildup.text: Pulse surveys are short, regular check-ins that capture real-time employee sentiment on specific topics.

discovery.text: They detect problems early—before frustration becomes disengagement and disengagement becomes turnover.

twist.text: Survey fatigue is real. Too many pulses without visible action make employees stop responding honestly—or at all.

climax.text: The key: always close the loop. Share results, explain what's changing, and act quickly on what matters most.

punchline.text: Ask often. Act faster. Otherwise, stop asking.

quiz.question: What causes survey fatigue?
options: Frequent surveys without visible follow-up action
